cloy: verb. To fill up or choke up; to stop up.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cony: noun. A rabbit, especially the European rabbit, Oryctolagus cuniculus (formerly known as Lepus cuniculus).
coo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Comox.
cool: adjective. Of a mildly low temperature.
loco: adverb. A direction in written or printed music to be returning to the proper pitch after having played an octave higher or lower.
loo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lombo.
loom: noun. A utensil; tool; a weapon; an article in general.
loon: noun. A crazy or deranged person; a lunatic.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
moly: noun. A magic herb or plant used by Odysseus to overcome Circe.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
yom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yombe.
yon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yongkom.
